title: [HW] Unequal equal types on instancing a module outputting a parametrized array / ArrayType sizeAttr defaults to 64bit
body:
I’ve recently been exposed to Circt at my student assistant job and have been playing around with it, seeing what kind of stuff the IR supports. During testing the inclusion of already written Verilog modules, I've run into an error with parameterized array types.

To reproduce the error, I've made the following simplified code:

```
hw.module.extern @submodule<A: i32 = 3, B: i32 = 8>(out out : !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">x!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>)

hw.module @test<A: i32 = 3, B: i32 = 8>(out out: !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">x!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>) {

  %out = hw.instance "submodule" @submodule<
    A: i32 = #hw.param.decl.ref<"A">, 
    B: i32 = #hw.param.decl.ref<"B">
  >() -> (out : !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">x!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>)

  hw.output %out : !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">x!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>
}
```

Running circt-opt results in an error, it seems to consider the two array types as unequal, even though the dumps sound equal.
```
/local/projects/circt_cnn/src/mlir/example_notworking.mlir:7:10: error: 'hw.instance' op result type #0 must be '!hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">xint<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>', but got '!hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">xint<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>'
  %out = hw.instance "submodule" @submodule<
         ^
/local/projects/circt_cnn/src/mlir/example_notworking.mlir:7:10: note: see current operation: %0 = "hw.instance"() {argNames = [], instanceName = "submodule", moduleName = @submodule, parameters = [#hw.param.decl<"A": i32 = #hw.param.decl.ref<"A">> : i32, #hw.param.decl<"B": i32 = #hw.param.decl.ref<"B">> : i32], resultNames = ["out"]} : () -> !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">xint<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>
/local/projects/circt_cnn/src/mlir/example_notworking.mlir:1:1: note: module declared here
hw.module.extern @submodule<A: i32 = 3, B: i32 = 8>(out out : !hw.array<#hw.param.decl.ref<"A">x!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"B">>>)
```

Attaching a debugger and looking at [InstanceImplementation.cpp#L140](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/2ec4cd09218b89b3ef35d9e3acfb0519e34da068/lib/Dialect/HW/InstanceImplementation.cpp#L140), I have found that the reason is the sizeAttr for the expected type and the result type being constructed differently. Both types have a `sizeAttr` with a StringAttr name "A", but the type of the `sizeAttr` is `i32` for the expected type and `i64` for the result typ. Switching the parameter A to be an `i64` instead did generate correct Verilog without any errors.

However I haven't been able to trace where exactly the attribute is constructed, due to the limitations with my debugger and probably not knowing enough about how CIRCT/MLIR work. Notably it is not in [evaluateParametricArrayType (HWAttributes.cpp#L1028](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/2ec4cd09218b89b3ef35d9e3acfb0519e34da068/lib/Dialect/HW/HWAttributes.cpp#L1028), changing that 64 to an 8 or 32 did not make a change in the error.

Maybe this error is intended behaviour, and the size of an array always has to be a i64 attribute. However, using i32 parameters as size works everywhere but in instancing a module. If it is intended, maybe an error should be implemented, like how the parametric !hw.int type only takes a i32 integer attribute and throws an error if you try to pass e.g. an i8 parameter ([HWTypes.cpp#L197](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/2ec4cd09218b89b3ef35d9e3acfb0519e34da068/lib/Dialect/HW/HWTypes.cpp#L197)).

If it's not intended behaviour, then wherever the size attribute for the result type of the instance is built needs to take into account the type of the parameter instead of making it a i64 by default.

Since I'm not that well versed in C++, if anyone has tips on how to debug things in CIRCT more cleany I'd also appreciate them, finding out what the issue was here took way too long thanks to fighting against the debugger and having to recompile the project often, see this [thread](https://discourse.llvm.org/t/help-debugging-circt-lldb-doesnt-find-half-the-names/73584). There are some other issues with parametric size arrays (e.g. `hw.array_get` crashing) I wanted to investigate a bit further before creating more issues.
